name: Great-Grandmother Rossi's Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the butter and sugar. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in the anise extract, then mix in the ground almonds and dry ingredients until combined.
2. Pour the batter into two buttered and floured cookie sheets, shaping it into a horseshoe shape.
3. Bake at 350°F for 15-18 minutes, or until set enough to cut.
4. Cut the baked mixture into one-inch pieces and place each on its side in the oven.
5. Continue baking at 225°F (or 250°F) for about two hours, or until the biscotti are as hard as desired. They can be stored in a jar for weeks and are perfect for dunking in coffee or milk.
